name: Brute Force: Password Guessing (DRY-RUN)
description: Credential Access / TA0006. The real technique is automated password guessing against an authentication endpoint. This descriptor is DRY-RUN ONLY: it prints the WOULD-attempt parameters (target 127.0.0.1:22, three candidate passwords from a stub list) and exits without invoking ssh, hydra, medusa, or any auth-attempt syscall. The runner's refuse-by-default gate is structural — even if the exec line invoked a real brute-force tool, the gate would still refuse without --unsafe-local.
exec: echo 'T1110.001 DRY-RUN: would brute-force 127.0.0.1:22 with 3 stub passwords'; echo '  candidate 1: password'; echo '  candidate 2: 123456'; echo '  candidate 3: admin'; echo 'no ssh/hydra/medusa invoked. no auth syscall made.'
